about.title: About us
about.vm_vision_text: The goal of the association "Albanian Community in Switzerland" is the organisation of the Albanian community towards the preservation and development of language, culture, art and national traditions, for all Albanians who live and work in the territory of the Swiss state, regardless of their region of origin, political convictions and confessional affiliation.
about.vm_mission_p1: To achieve the above-mentioned goal, the "Albanian Community in Switzerland", as an umbrella organisation, uses all legal forms of organisation such as meetings, assemblies, events, congresses, symposia and other organisational forms that mainly concern the coordination of Albanian community activities at cantonal and federal level. One of the most important instruments that BSHZ is working to realise are the Albanian Cultural Centres, centres that are planned in several cantons of Switzerland.
about.vm_mission_p2: Another important element for achieving the vision is cooperation with the respective institutions of Switzerland, Kosovo, Albania, North Macedonia and Albanian municipalities in Serbia and Montenegro - institutions that are believed to be willing to cooperate in the preservation and cultivation of Albanian national values.
about.vm_mission_p3: Another important element is cooperation with various associations and personalities in Switzerland, in the homeland and worldwide, who share the same vision regarding the organisation of the Albanian diaspora as a Community (Gemeinschaft - Communaute - Comunita - Community).

insurance.title: Collective Insurance
insurance.intro: To achieve an agreement on health insurance premium reductions for its members and supporters, the "Albanian Community in Switzerland" contacted the most reputable insurance companies for quality and services over several years. After numerous meetings and discussions based on the "AGS – Kollektiv" project by the "Albanian Community in Switzerland", an agreement was signed with the Helsana Group in 2008!
insurance.discount: Passive members – supporters of the association "Albanian Community in Switzerland" who are insured or wish to be insured with one of the Helsana Group insurances can request a 20% discount through the number KN30001072 assigned to the "Albanian Community in Switzerland". The discount can be requested from insurance advisors or directly at a Helsana Group branch.
insurance.how_to: To benefit from this discount, it is sufficient to prove the status of passive member or supporter of the association "Albanian Community in Switzerland". The form proving passive membership status can be requested via email at info@bshz.ch or filled out when signing the contract with HELSANA insurance.
insurance.financing: The "Albanian Community in Switzerland" as an umbrella organisation (Dachverband) is financed by sponsors, donors and through the realisation of various projects for the benefit of the Albanian community in Switzerland!
insurance.note: Note: Only active members of the association have financial obligations, not passive members – supporters!
insurance.companies_text: Insurance companies wishing to obtain the right to grant the 20% discount to members and supporters of the "Albanian Community in Switzerland" must first obtain a signed and stamped cooperation certificate from the association. Interested insurance companies can write to us at E-mail: info@bshz.ch and contact us at Tel: 043 5267211 ; 076 3880957

albanians.title: Albanians in Switzerland
albanians.community_text: Albanians form one of the largest foreign communities in Switzerland, with around 250,000 people of Albanian origin living in the country. This includes citizens from Kosovo, Albania, North Macedonia and other Albanian-speaking regions. The community is spread across all of Switzerland, with particular concentrations in the German-speaking cantons such as Zurich, Bern, St. Gallen, Aargau and Lucerne.
albanians.history_text: The migration of Albanians to Switzerland began in the 1960s with seasonal workers, mainly from Kosovo and North Macedonia. A second migration phase occurred in the 1980s and 1990s, when many Albanians fled due to political repression and conflicts in the former Yugoslavia. The Kosovo War (1998-1999) brought a large wave of refugees, many of whom settled permanently in Switzerland. Today, the second and third generations are well integrated into Swiss society, making significant contributions in economic, cultural and sporting fields.
albanians.integration_text: Albanians in Switzerland are known for their strong work ethic and active contribution to society. In sports, many footballers of Albanian origin have represented Switzerland at the international level, becoming ambassadors of successful integration. In business, many Albanians have founded successful enterprises in various sectors, creating jobs and contributing to the Swiss economy. In culture, numerous festivals, organizations and events promote the rich Albanian heritage while building bridges between the two cultures.
albanians.bshz_text: The Albanian Community in Switzerland (BSHZ) is the leading organization representing the interests of the Albanian community in Switzerland. Founded with the aim of strengthening the voice of Albanians in Swiss society, the BSHZ works in several key areas: promoting integration while preserving cultural identity, representing the community in dialogue with Swiss institutions, organizing cultural and educational activities, and supporting young people in their professional and personal development. The BSHZ is committed to harmonious coexistence and strengthening ties between the Albanian community and Swiss society.
albanians.countries_list: Kosovo, Albania, North Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia
albanians.cantons_list: Zurich, Bern, St. Gallen, Aargau, Lucerne

culture.title: Cultural Centres
culture.intro_1: The idea of establishing Albanian Cultural Centres in Switzerland is an old idea which has often been mentioned as a necessity by many activists of national culture, but nothing concrete is known to have been undertaken.
culture.intro_2: Starting from the difficult situation of Albanian cultural associations, where the leaders of associations, artistic directors and membership, instead of dealing with cultural matters, face various infrastructural and financial problems every month, in 2018 the Albanian Community in Switzerland took the initiative to establish a FOUNDATION which would deal with the construction and financing of Cultural Centres and cultural activities in these Albanian Cultural Centres in Switzerland.
culture.intro_3: The issue of preserving and developing culture, language, art and national traditions in the diaspora has simply been left as if it were a private matter of a few activists and not a national concern! Albanian activists who for decades have been and continue to be involved in preserving, cultivating and developing Albanian culture, who have tried to create Cultural Centres with all the sections that a Cultural Centre should have, have been deeply disappointed by the behaviour of the community which is currently oriented only towards the construction of religious buildings and by the attitude of the countries of origin which are not at all cooperative in this direction!
culture.intro_4: Starting from this reality, the idea of establishing the "FOUNDATION OF ALBANIAN CULTURAL CENTRES IN SWITZERLAND" emerged as a necessity to overcome this unfavourable cultural situation of the Albanian community in Switzerland!
culture.vision_1: The vision is the preservation and development of culture among the younger generations with the aim of integration, preservation and development of Albanian culture, language and traditions of the Albanian community in Switzerland!
culture.vision_2: The ultimate goal is that through education and inspiration of Albanian youth with all the national spiritual wealth of Albanian culture, the diaspora youth should continuously be prepared to make plans and projects for return from and investment in the homeland, with the MISSION of building and strengthening our ethnic states and territories!
culture.vision_3: It is high time that the Albanian community in Switzerland begins creating a reliable system of protection against assimilation, a system of preservation, cultivation and development of national values. We are confident that the Albanian community in Switzerland, through the system of Cultural Centres, will be able to create conditions to educate generations of Albanians who, with willpower, knowledge and Western professional preparation, will continuously, generation after generation, help our ethnic states and territories become politically and economically functional and stable!
culture.project_title: Conceptual Project of the Cultural Centre
culture.project_intro: The description text of an Albanian Cultural Centre in Switzerland, on the basis of which the Conceptual Project was developed:
culture.project_items: The maximum number of visitors frequenting the Centre is expected to be around 50,000 people/year!|The maximum number of seats with tables is around 500 people, while without tables - only with chairs around 1,000 people.|The first Albanian Cultural Centre in Switzerland will have a hall laid with parquet flooring which will be used for various cultural and sporting exercises, for assemblies and events, for national and family celebrations.|The hall will have the main stage where dancing can take place and concerts and theatrical performances can be given. The stage will be raised above the floor according to standards with spaces backstage for dressing-preparation of artists as well as space for placing equipment.|At the entrance, the Centre will have a reception hall with all the conditions for reception and sufficient toilets for men and women.|It is planned that the Cultural Centre will have a restaurant with around 200 seats.|The Cultural Centre will have a library and a reading room with 50 seats with tables.|The Cultural Centre will have a museum space where exhibits of importance to the history, culture and national traditions of Albania will be displayed.|The Cultural Centre will have two spaces equipped for various courses and seminars to be organised at the Cultural Centre. Two adequate spaces for courses and seminars with 50 seats each and a space where lecturers can make preparations and take breaks.|The Cultural Centre will have an office for the needs of the Presidency - management of the Cultural Centre.|The Centre will have a workshop (room) where all tools and equipment for the maintenance of the Centre will be kept.|Within the Cultural Centre, a shop will also operate selling products from Albanian producers from Albania, Kosovo, Macedonia, the Presevo Valley, Montenegro as well as various producers from the Albanian diaspora. This space, like the restaurant, will be rented out.|The Cultural Centre will have a maintenance employee - caretaker (Hauswart - Concierge).|The Cultural Centre will have bedrooms for guests of the Centre.|In the courtyard of the Cultural Centre there must be parking space for around 100 cars and sufficient space for unobstructed movement of people.|The Albanian Cultural Centres in Switzerland will bear the names of heroes, martyrs and distinguished figures from the glorious history and creativity of Albanian history.
culture.arch_author: By architect Florent Berisha - "Conceptual Project of the First Albanian Cultural Centre in Switzerland"
culture.arch_intro: This project, the first of its kind, was realised on the basis of the above text and on the basis of supplementary explanations from the association president dipl. eng. Ahmet Asani.
culture.arch_phase: The first phase of planning and analysis for this building was challenging given the importance of this cultural building of national significance! The mission of this building was a motivation to offer the most sustainable, functional and aesthetic solutions for the building.
culture.parcel_text: The plot where the project is designed is a flat terrain, the space used (designed) on this plot is 56 ares (5,600 m²). The access and exit routes for pedestrians and vehicles consist of two arteries, one for entry to the building and one for exit.
culture.parcel_zones: According to the concept, the space is divided into 2 zones:
culture.parcel_zone1: Building, Square (Statue, Green space, flowers, Promenade)
culture.building_text: The Cultural Centre building has the main entrance which can be easily perceived from the volumetry and does not create confusion for visitors on their first approach to the building. The building also contains a secondary entrance for officials, artists, students, seminar participants, as well as an emergency entrance.
culture.building_facade: Regarding the facade and architectural form, we have tried to convey the modern spirit of architecture by designing a facade with a repetitive rhythm and regular openings which create a good effect on visual perception and at the same time sufficient lighting.
culture.ground_text_1: At the entrance we have the hall accompanied by reception and the cloakroom section. As part of the hall there are toilets for women and men.
culture.ground_text_2: The ground floor includes one of the main spaces where the multi-purpose hall is laid with wooden parquet, accompanied by the stage and the viewing and make-up spaces for artists. The hall capacity is for 400 people with tables and approximately 1,000 seats without tables. Due to easier access and more frequent use, the ground floor also houses the restaurant with kitchen and sufficient space for various gatherings. On the ground floor there is also a premises which can serve as a business premises in the field of finance or insurance or as a shop where various products can be sold.
culture.floor1_text_1: Through the main hall and the rear hall we ascend to the first floor where, through the C-shaped corridor, the spaces are arranged which also have considerable use and various activities. On the first floor, the offices for the Centre's presidency are planned, a space that offers good comfort accompanied by: a meeting room for the presidency, archives, waiting hall and WC.
culture.floor1_text_2: As part of the activities on the first floor there are: the Gallery for various exhibitions, the Museum where various artefacts, objects and other traditional Albanian items can be placed, the conference room with 50 seats which will serve for various conferences, seminars, lectures, promotions, etc. Also in the spaces of the first floor, 5 rooms are planned, two of which can serve as guest bedrooms and three others as offices that can be rented out!
culture.floor2_text_1: On the second floor, the corridor circulation line has been respected by organising rooms along the entire corridor. The second floor also has a large number of spaces for various activities mainly intended for book lovers, readers, pupils and students.
culture.floor2_text_2: As an idea, three classrooms with 50 seats each are planned where various courses, seminars, etc. can be held. On the second floor, the library and reading room are located, spaces which offer good conditions for studying and group work. Also on the second floor there is a space of 190 m² where dance courses, acting classes, etc. can be held.

statuti.title: Program and Statutes
statuti.program_subtitle: Excerpts from the Program and Statutes
statuti.program_intro: The "Albanian Community in Switzerland" (hereinafter BSHZ) is formed with the aim of expressing, protecting and realising the interests, rights and obligations of all Albanian emigrants from all Albanian ethnic territories who live and work in Switzerland.
statuti.sections: BSHZ, in cooperation with the state entities and institutions of the Swiss state and those in the homeland, will engage in creating conditions for the fastest possible integration and advancement of the Albanian diaspora in Switzerland.|BSHZ works continuously in finding and developing the best organisational forms for preserving the identity, culture and national traditions of Albanians and at the same time the most effective integration of the Albanian diaspora in Switzerland. BSHZ works on building and maintaining national consciousness, getting to know, bringing closer and uniting emigrants among themselves.|BSHZ, in cooperation with state institutions in the homeland and those in Switzerland, will engage in the development and enhancement of cooperation in the spheres of economy, culture, sport, humanitarian affairs and other fields as needed.|BSHZ, through its work and activity, will develop and deepen cooperation and friendship with similar Swiss bodies and associations in all fields of mutual interest and benefit by developing and organising joint programmes and events.|BSHZ, through its humanitarian, solidarity and national activities, has an important role not only in presenting and affirming the national cause, but will institutionally and professionally counter abuses and misinformation that damage our national cause.|BSHZ engages in taking measures to prevent negative phenomena such as: delinquency, drug addiction, criminality, various conflicts and other deviant behaviours. BSHZ engages in creating the institutional and spiritual connection of the diaspora with the homeland.|BSHZ engages in strengthening organisational capacities for the most efficient fulfilment of all obligations towards itself as a diaspora and towards the homeland as a whole. For the realisation of its projects, BSHZ engages proven activists of our national cause in the diaspora, professional individuals, distinguished intellectuals and activists with decades of experience in diaspora affairs. BSHZ will primarily engage cadres from the Albanian diaspora who have completed their education or specialised and have work experience in Swiss firms and institutions in its Professional Councils.|BSHZ engages in having activists in its presidency regardless of gender, faith, or political convictions; what matters is that they are patriots with many years of experience and deep democratic convictions and are ready to act.|BSHZ believes that organising the diaspora on party, sectarian, clan and other divisive rather than unifying bases is outdated. BSHZ appeals to the state institutions of Kosovo, Albania and Albanian entities in Macedonia, Montenegro and the Presevo Valley that any form of organisation which divides and fragments the Albanian emigration should not be supported.
statuti.history_text: The drafting of the Statutes of the "Albanian Community in Switzerland" was made by the decision of the Founding Council of the association "Albanian Community for Switzerland", dated 31.08.1998, in Bern, Switzerland. The statutes of the association "Albanian Community in Switzerland" were amended and approved for the first time on 04.04.1999 at the Founding Assembly of BSHZ in Bern, Switzerland! The proposal for amending the statutes for the second time came from the president and the Presidency of BSHZ, after the declaration of independence of Kosovo. Discussions regarding the necessary statutory changes were public and lasted until 30.03.2011.
statuti.current_statute: At the Assembly of 04.04.2011 held in Arbon, Switzerland, the necessary statutory changes of the association were made and as such the Statutes are in force. BSHZ has the aim of seeking, finding, preserving and coordinating the common interests of Albanians in Switzerland. BSHZ operates through Cantonal Communities, associations, clubs, business organisations and activists who engage in the realisation of the programme and projects of BSHZ.
statuti.cooperation: BSHZ cooperates with all Swiss state institutions and those of the states of Kosovo and Albania, with the representatives of Albanians in Macedonia, Montenegro and the Presevo Valley and with all organisations that are willing to help in the realisation of the vision and mission of BSHZ! The formulation of the BSHZ Statutes is based on the fundamental provisions of the BSHZ Programme. The drafting of the Statutes is based on and harmonised with the Swiss Civil Code, specifically with the provisions of articles 60-72. All matters not foreseen by the statutes are regulated by special regulations.
